title: T-001 — Lock OEE permission enum (oee.*.* RBAC baseline, OEE §3, §15.3)
prompt:
# T-001 — Lock OEE permission enum

PRD: docs/prd/15-OEE-PRD.md §3 (Personas & Roles), §15.3 (Admin screens), §11 V-OEE-ACCESS-2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Append a delimited OEE permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering target/override/export/anomaly/big-loss/shift-pattern strings per PRD §3 RBAC matrix and Auditor A's OEE-008 slice. Export `ALL_OEE_PERMISSIONS`. Per Foundation §3 model is flat dot-namespaced strings (no derived matrix).

## Implementation contract
1. Append exactly these 13 strings (regex `^oee\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`) to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` under an `OEE_PERMISSIONS` group with JSDoc citing PRD §3 + §15.3:
   - `oee.dashboard.read`
   - `oee.target.edit`
   - `oee.override.create`, `oee.override.delete`
   - `oee.export.csv`, `oee.export.pdf`
   - `oee.anomaly.acknowledge`
   - `oee.big_loss.map_edit`
   - `oee.shift_pattern.edit`, `oee.shift_pattern.read`
   - `oee.downtime.annotate`, `oee.downtime.escalate`
   - `oee.tv.kiosk_view`
2. Export `ALL_OEE_PERMISSIONS` as a typed `Permission[]` array literal so vitest can iterate.
3. Add vitest covering: (a) presence of all 13 strings, (b) regex `^oee\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` match, (c) no duplicates across the full Permission enum union, (d) CODEOWNERS still architect-locks the file.

## Files
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [modify]
- CODEOWNERS [modify if missing entry]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the enum file is read, when imported, then it exposes exactly the 13 oee.*.* strings listed in the prompt under an `OEE_PERMISSIONS` group.
2. Given a permission key matches `^oee\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when the enum is parsed, then no duplicates exist (vitest assertion across full Permission union).
3. Given the file is changed, when CODEOWNERS is read, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` remains architect-locked.
4. Given `ALL_OEE_PERMISSIONS` is imported, when iterated, then it returns exactly 13 entries typed `Permission[]`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: extend permissions.test.ts asserting the 13 strings + regex + uniqueness; run vitest, expect failure.
- GREEN: append strings + export; re-run.
- Typecheck + lint must pass.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts && pnpm typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not remove or rename any existing permission string (would break ESLint enum-lock guard from Foundation T-046).
- Do not introduce a parallel matrix model (Foundation §3 [D2] flat model only).
- Do not weaken CODEOWNERS coverage.
- Do not add maintenance/quality/production-namespaced perms in this task — out of scope.

pipeline_inputs.description: Lock the 13 oee.*.* permission strings into packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts per PRD §3 RBAC matrix and Auditor A OEE-008 slice.
pipeline_inputs.details: Append OEE_PERMISSIONS group with JSDoc citing PRD §3 line numbers. Export ALL_OEE_PERMISSIONS typed array. Tests assert regex `^oee\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, presence of all 13, no duplicates across full enum, and CODEOWNERS still gates the file. Cross-cuts: closes Auditor A OEE-008 (permissions enum delta); enables T-010..T-022 Server Actions and route guards to import strongly-typed strings instead of literals. Roles `oee_viewer`, `oee_supervisor`, `oee_admin` (PRD §3) consume these strings server-side via the existing role-permission mapping table in 02-SETTINGS.

Do not change foundation app.current_org_id() function
